text: fat is 4.5g. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
carbs is 78g. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
protein is 12.9g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
vitamin e at 1% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 36% DV, iron at 17% DV, zinc at 25% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

on the plus side. useful staple energy source because that matters because grains are often judged by how practical they are in real diets. works as a meal anchor because a lot of value comes from being easy to build around. usually affordable and scalable because practicality matters for staple foods.

the drawbacks next. protein quality is limited because even the better grains usually need pairing. carb load is still the main burden because that is the category risk no grain fully escapes. quality can drop fast with processing because the grain category punishes refinement pretty hard.
B tier. Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
